faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about glamping properties, feasibility studies, and outdoor hospitality

items.question: What is a glamping feasibility study?
items.answer: A glamping feasibility study evaluates whether a proposed glamping property will be financially viable and operationally successful. It includes market analysis, financial projections, site assessment, and competitive analysis to help investors make informed decisions about their outdoor hospitality investment.

items.question: How do I find glamping properties near me?
items.answer: Use our interactive map to search for glamping properties by location. We have 600+ verified properties across the United States and Canada. You can filter by location, property type, amenities, and price range to find the perfect glamping destination for your outdoor adventure.

items.question: What's the difference between glamping and camping?
items.answer: Glamping (glamorous camping) combines the outdoor experience of camping with the comfort and amenities of a hotel. Unlike traditional camping, glamping accommodations typically include comfortable beds, electricity, heating, and often private bathrooms and kitchens. It offers nature immersion without sacrificing modern conveniences.

items.question: How much does a glamping feasibility study cost?
items.answer: Glamping feasibility studies typically cost between $5,000 and $15,000, depending on the scope, property size, and complexity. Factors include market research depth, financial modeling requirements, and site assessment needs. More comprehensive studies with detailed analysis may cost $20,000 or more.

items.question: What should I look for in a glamping property?
items.answer: Key factors include location and accessibility, unique accommodation types, quality amenities (wifi, bathrooms, kitchen facilities), safety features, and proximity to attractions. Our database includes detailed information on 600+ properties including ratings, amenities, and pricing to help you compare options.

items.question: Are glamping properties profitable?
items.answer: Profitability varies significantly based on location, occupancy rates, pricing strategy, and operational costs. A professional feasibility study can provide market-specific revenue projections and help determine if a property will be profitable in your target market. Many successful glamping properties achieve 60-80% occupancy rates with strong seasonal demand.

anchorPointInsights.subtitleWithAnchor: Properties near {anchorName} — rates by distance, occupancy, and seasonal data.
anchorPointInsights.avgWinterRateTooltip: Based on {withRates} of {total} units with winter rates
anchorPointInsights.topProperties: Top Properties by Winter Rate (Within 30 mi)
anchorPointInsights.topPropertiesX: Top Properties by Winter Rate (Within {distance} mi)
anchorPointInsights.noPropertiesWithin30: No properties with winter rates within 30 mi
anchorPointInsights.noPropertiesWithinX: No properties with winter rates within {distance} mi
anchorPointInsights.noInsightsData: No insights data available. Ensure anchor tables (ski_resorts or national-parks) and property tables are populated.

siteDesign.title: RV Site Setup
siteDesign.subtitle: Yield and economics calculator for RV park site layout optimization. Estimate site count, annual revenue, and revenue per acre from parcel and site-type inputs.
siteDesign.roadWidthHelp: Estimated % of land for roads. Wider roads (18–40 ft) reduce site yield. Formula: ~12% base + 0.8% per ft over 18.
siteDesign.countHelp: Leave all blank to auto-fill with the highest revenue-per-sq-ft type.
siteDesign.autoFilledExplanation: With no counts entered, the parcel is filled with the site type that has the highest revenue per sq ft. This maximizes total revenue for the given land.
siteDesign.partialFillExplanation: Remaining land after your entered counts was filled with the best-performing type among those with no count.
siteDesign.overCapacityWarning: Over capacity by {sqft} sq ft ({acres} acres). Reduce counts to fit parcel.
siteDesign.operatingNightsHelp: 365 for year-round. Seasonal parks: e.g. 180 (6 months).
siteDesign.devCostHelp: Development cost per site. Basic: $15–30K, Mid: $30–60K, Premium: $60–100K+.
siteDesign.capRateHelp: Typical 7–12% for RV parks. Used to estimate value.
siteDesign.campspotNoData: No Campspot data available for this state.
siteDesign.autoFillRemainingLandHelp: When counts are partially entered, fill remaining land with the best revenue-per-sq-ft type.
siteDesign.shareWarningSitesOmitted: This configuration is too large to fully include in the URL. Some site-type details were omitted from the share link.

siteBuilder.title: Site Builder
siteBuilder.subtitle: Configure glamping units and RV sites with amenities, calculate costs from Cost Explorer data, and generate one illustrative hero image per configuration row (quantity does not multiply renders).
siteBuilder.unitTypeFromCatalog: Glamping unit type matches your Unit Manufacturers selection. Clear the catalog unit to change it.
siteBuilder.qualityTierFromCatalog: Quality tier is locked while a catalog unit is selected; base cost uses the catalog price. Clear the catalog unit to change tier.
siteBuilder.amenityPickerSummary: {count, plural, one {# amenity selected} other {# amenities selected}}
siteBuilder.amenityPickerSummaryWithOverrides: {count, plural, one {# amenity} other {# amenities}} · {overrides, plural, one {# custom cost} other {# custom costs}}
siteBuilder.amenityRowCostHelp: Costs you edit here apply only to this row in Site Builder. Catalog defaults stay on the Amenities page.
siteBuilder.amenitySelectedEmpty: No amenities selected yet. Use the field above to search and add.
siteBuilder.amenityUnitCostsHelp: Default values come from feasibility studies when available, otherwise from researched typical installed costs. Adjust any row to match your project; overrides apply to this session only and are included in cost totals and Excel export.
siteBuilder.amenitiesPageSubtitle: One table lists Site Builder catalog costs and all_glamping_properties amenity fields. Level is Property (resort), Unit (glamping unit / model), or Site (RV pad & hookups). Cost and Applies to are editable for every row; use Save to persist.
siteBuilder.amenitiesAppliesHintGlamping: Shown only on glamping unit rows.
siteBuilder.amenitiesAppliesHintRv: Shown only on RV site rows.
siteBuilder.amenitiesAppliesHintBoth: Shown on both glamping and RV rows.
siteBuilder.amenitiesNoRows: No amenities found. Apply Site Builder migrations, run scripts/seed-site-builder-data.ts, then scripts/populate-amenities-glamping-metadata.ts (or re-run seed, which syncs glamping metadata).
siteBuilder.amenitiesTableHint: {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}} — catalog slugs and dataset columns.
siteBuilder.amenitiesTableHintFiltered: Showing {filtered, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}} of {total, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}.
siteBuilder.amenitiesFilterEmpty: No rows match this resort type. Choose All or another option.
siteBuilder.amenitiesUnsavedCount: {count, plural, one {# unsaved row} other {# unsaved rows}}
siteBuilder.generateImagesDescription: This may take 20–45 seconds per image.
siteBuilder.imageLocationHelp: Used in the prompt as the setting so the model can match region, terrain, and general environment to your study.
siteBuilder.imageDescriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Red exterior, large windows; say if internal roads are gravel, aggregate, DG, or dirt (otherwise paved is assumed).
siteBuilder.sceneFramingHelp: Only changes the first “Scene:” line in the image prompt. Use Generic, Marina, or Campground when the study context is not a glamping or RV resort—row types still control the unit or site rendered.
siteBuilder.sceneFraming_auto: Auto (from rows: glamping-only, RV-only, or mixed)
siteBuilder.imageCatalogIllustrativeNote: Catalog-linked rows: product-page images are fetched best-effort (often missing). Prompts still use the product URL; treat output as illustrative, not an exact manufacturer match.
siteBuilder.imageReproducibilityNote: Outputs can vary between runs even with a fixed seed—providers do not guarantee pixel-identical repeats for audits or before/after comparisons.
siteBuilder.imageBatchContinuityResetNote: After a failed render, the reference chain was reset: the next successful image no longer continues from the last good image, so batch order and on-screen continuity may not match “2 of 4” style counters until you regenerate in sequence.

imageBatchStyleHint.summary: Batch-wide defaults keep framing consistent: one hero per row, 4:3, midday lighting; internal roads are paved unless Additional details say otherwise.
imageBatchStyleHint.intro: These options apply to every image in this batch and keep framing consistent.
bullets: One hero per row (not per Quantity); from the second image on, each chains from the last successful render—same property, new unit/site as hero and a new angle.
Time of day (lighting) is fixed to neutral midday.
Internal roads default to paved unless Additional details clearly describe unpaved, gravel, aggregate, DG, or dirt circulation.

siteBuilder.exportImagesDocxError: Could not build the Word file. Try fewer images or download individually.
siteBuilder.noCatalogUnits: No catalog units found. Run extraction from Cost Explorer.

sitesExport.title: Sites export
sitesExport.subtitle: Filter market tables and download a Sage-format sites file (XLSX or CSV).
sitesExport.regionFiltersDisabledHint: Clear zip code and range (miles) to filter by country or state. Proximity search does not use these filters.
sitesExport.exportProgressOvertime: Past the rough estimate—large exports can still take several more minutes.
sitesExport.exportGatewayTimeout: The server stopped waiting before the export finished. Narrow filters, try CSV (faster than XLSX), or export a smaller slice.
sitesExport.exportNetworkError: The connection dropped or was reset. Check your network and try again.
sitesExport.dbQueryTimeout: The database timed out. Try a smaller zip radius, fewer sources, or narrower filters.
sitesExport.countLimitExceeded: Too many rows match these filters. Try a smaller area (zip + miles), fewer sources, or country/state filters.
sitesExport.noSources: Select at least one data source.
sitesExport.geoZipRadiusHint: Enter both a zip code and a range in miles, or leave both empty.
sitesExport.regionScopeRequired: Select at least one country or state, or set zip code and range (miles). Without that, the export would include all markets (very large downloads).

discoveryPipeline.title: Discovery Pipeline (RSS & News)
discoveryPipeline.subtitle: New glamping resorts found and added from RSS feeds, Tavily search, and article processing

rvIndustryOverview.title: RV Industry Overview
rvIndustryOverview.subtitle: 2025 regional metrics from Campspot (ARDR, avg. retail daily rate, and occupancy). Map shows the contiguous U.S. with Alaska in the lower-left (Albers USA) and Hawaii as a small Mercator inset immediately to the left of Alaska; both are included in West region totals.
rvIndustryOverview.unitFilterHint: All charts below use only Campspot rows classified as the selected type, except the two unit-type comparison bar charts (avg. rate by type and mix by type), which always include Glamping, RV Site, and Tent Site together, and except “RV Site Types Distribution & Rates,” which always uses the RV Sites cohort. Unclassified rows are excluded from each slice.
rvIndustryOverview.standardFiltersIntro: The same row-level rules run everywhere on this page for the selected unit type. A value is only averaged or counted when it falls inside the standard band for that metric. There is no percentile winsorizing beyond these fixed bounds.
rvIndustryOverview.standardFiltersOccupancy: Occupancy (any year used in a chart): after parsing (fractions 0–1 are scaled to percent), include only values from 10% through 99% inclusive. Values below 10% or at 100% are excluded as unreliable or invalid.
rvIndustryOverview.standardFiltersRate: Retail daily rate / ARDR (USD): include only values from $10 through $3,000 per night inclusive for whichever field that chart uses (2024 annual avg. retail daily rate, 2025 avg. retail daily rate, or seasonal weekday/weekend columns on season charts). Retail Daily Rate YTD is not used on this page.
rvIndustryOverview.standardFiltersPropertyCohort: Property- and RV-only charts (amenity penetration, RV parking mix, unit-type distribution): a row is included only when 2025 occupancy and 2025 ARDR from the average retail daily rate 2025 column both pass the standard bands, so denominators stay aligned with rate-based charts.
rvIndustryOverview.standardFiltersByChart: Regional map coloring uses 2025 ARDR from the average retail daily rate 2025 column (same as the state choropleth) and 2025 occupancy, each in band (Lodging uses a higher ARDR ceiling on the regional map only). State hover/pop-up matched cohort requires 2024 and 2025 occupancy and ARDR (annual columns) each in band. Trends, resort size, unit-type rate, season and surface rate bars, and amenity ADR comparisons apply the band to every occupancy or rate value as it enters that chart’s buckets.
rvIndustryOverview.mapCaption: Unweighted means across Campspot rows with both 2025 ARDR from the average retail daily rate 2025 column and 2025 occupancy populated, after standard occupancy and ARDR filtering (regional ARDR band is $10–$3k for RV/Tent and $10–$10k for Lodging).
rvIndustryOverview.mapInteractHint: Hover a state for a quick view of matched-cohort 2024 vs 2025 averages, or click for full metrics and changes.
rvIndustryOverview.rowsScannedDetail: That count is every Campspot row read in this pass, up to the server row cap, even when a row does not qualify for the map averages.
rvIndustryOverview.mapOutliers: Fixed-band filtering (not percentile-based): occupancy must be between 10% and 99% (100% excluded); retail rates must be between $10 and $3,000 USD for the field that chart uses. See “Standard data filters” below for chart-by-chart detail.
rvIndustryOverview.mapRegionalInclusion: Regional 2025 map averages include only rows whose state maps to one of the five regions, whose 2025 occupancy parses as a non-negative number (values from 0–1 are treated as fractions and scaled to percent) and lies between 10% and 99% (100% excluded), and whose 2025 ARDR from the average retail daily rate 2025 column lies between $10 and $3,000 for RV and Tent, or between $10 and $10,000 for Lodging (glamping). Retail Daily Rate YTD and seasonal columns are not used for these regional averages. Currency symbols and thousands separators in text fields are normalized before parsing. Rows are excluded if the state is missing or outside those regions, or if occupancy or the 2025 rate is blank, “no data”, non-numeric, or outside the applicable bands.
rvIndustryOverview.mapStateCohortNote: Per-state hover and pop-up metrics use a matched cohort: only Campspot rows with valid occupancy and ARDR in both 2024 and 2025, each in the standard bands. For ARDR, both years use the average retail daily rate columns (2024 and 2025). The regional map and state ADR shading use the same 2025 average retail daily rate column as the five-region labels.

mapState.tooltipCohortHint: Matched cohort: same rows with valid occupancy and ARDR in both years (see details for ARDR rules).
mapState.noData: No Campspot rows in this state qualify for the matched cohort (valid occupancy and ARDR in both 2024 and 2025, with the same ARDR rules as the trends chart).
mapState.methodNote: Unweighted means over the matched cohort only. ARDR for both 2024 and 2025 uses the average retail daily rate columns for those years (Retail Daily Rate YTD is not used).
mapState.matchedCohortNote: Year-over-year change compares the same set of rows: units without both years are excluded so new 2025-only reporting does not shift the averages.

trends.chartTitle: RV Resort / Park Occupancy Rate Trends
trends.intro: Current and historical trends (2024–2025 only). 2024 uses full-year occupancy and ARDR (avg. retail daily rate). For 2025, ARDR uses the average retail daily rate 2025 column only; occupancy uses the 2025 occupancy field. The U.S. column pools all regions. No 2023 data is included.
trends.methodology: Unweighted means across Campspot rows that have both metrics for each cohort, after standard occupancy and ARDR filtering for the year being averaged. U.S. aggregates include every qualifying row in the five regions.
trends.loadError: Could not load trends chart data.

sizeImpact.chartTitle: Impact of Resort Size on ARDR & Occupancy
sizeImpact.intro: Avg. retail daily rates: pricing is firmer, and ARDR increased from full-year 2024 to full-year 2025 in each size band in this sample. The Northeast and largest parks tend to lead on rate; value-oriented bands remain comparatively lower. Overall, the pattern suggests stable occupancy with improving pricing power where 2025 data is available.
sizeImpact.body: ARDR (avg. retail daily rate): larger parks typically carry a rate premium versus smaller bands; the table below shows live Campspot means for each size tier (2024 full year vs 2025 full year). Resort size uses property total sites when present, otherwise quantity of units. Large is 100+ sites, medium 50–99, small 25–49 (other counts are excluded).
sizeImpact.tableFootnote: ARDR change is percent change from 2024 to 2025. Occupancy and 2025 ARDR use Campspot full-year fields (occupancy_rate_2025, avg_retail_daily_rate_2025), with standard occupancy and ARDR band filtering applied per year.
sizeImpact.loadError: Could not load resort size chart data.

unitTypeCharts.loadError: Could not load unit type chart data.
unitTypeCharts.notAffectedByUnitFilter: Not filtered by the RV Sites / Tent Sites / Lodging toggle above; all three unit types are compared together.

unitTypeByRate.subtitle: Avg. Retail Daily Rate Before Taxes and Booking Fees
unitTypeByRate.intro: Mean 2025 ARDR (avg. retail daily rate) by classified unit type across Campspot rows in the five U.S. regions. Rows need a parseable unit type (Glamping, RV Site, or Tent Site), state in-region, and a positive average retail daily rate 2025.
unitTypeByRate.methodology: Unweighted mean of row-level rates within each type. Rows must pass standard 2025 occupancy and 2025 ARDR (average retail daily rate 2025 column) bands. No taxes or booking-fee columns are added; values reflect the same retail rate fields used elsewhere on this page.
unitTypeByRate.noData: No rows matched the cohort for this chart.

unitTypeDistribution.subtitle: Share of sites by unit type, averaged across properties
unitTypeDistribution.intro: For each property (name, state, city), site counts use quantity of units when at least 1, otherwise 1 per row. Mix percentages are computed from classified Glamping / RV / Tent rows only, then averaged across properties with at least one classified site. Each property counts equally in that average: this is not the same as total glamping sites divided by total RV sites nationwide (a small bar here can still reflect many glamping sites if they sit on few properties).
unitTypeDistribution.methodology: Classification order matches the RV Industry Overview unit-type rules (same priority as glamping market unit tagging). Only rows in the five U.S. regions are included, with standard 2025 occupancy and 2025 ARDR (average retail daily rate 2025 column) band filtering applied before counting site mix. The three bar values are means of property-level mixes and always sum to about 100%; they answer “what is the typical property’s mix?” not “what share of all unit-rows are glamping?”
unitTypeDistribution.noData: No properties matched the cohort for this chart.

ratesBySeason.subtitle: Avg. Retail Daily Rate Before Taxes and Booking Fees
ratesBySeason.intro: Unweighted mean of each seasonal rate column across Campspot rows in the five U.S. regions. A row contributes to a bar only when that column parses within the standard retail rate band ($10–$3,000).
ratesBySeason.methodology: Fields: winter_weekday, winter_weekend, spring_weekday, spring_weekend, summer_weekday, summer_weekend, fall_weekday, fall_weekend. Each value must fall in the standard ARDR band. No taxes or booking-fee columns are added.
ratesBySeason.noData: No seasonal rate values matched the cohort for this chart.
ratesBySeason.loadError: Could not load seasonal rates chart data.

siteSurfaceRates.subtitle: Avg. Retail Daily Rates Are Before Taxes and Booking Fees
siteSurfaceRates.intro: Mean 2025 ARDR (avg. retail daily rate) by RV site surface type for Campspot rows in the selected unit-type slice (same toggle as the rest of the page). Rows must be in the five U.S. regions, have a surface type that maps to Concrete Pad, Loose Gravel, or Grass or Field, and average retail daily rate 2025 inside the standard ARDR band. A dash means no qualifying rows for that bucket (often `rv_surface_type` is blank on non-RV rows, e.g. Lodging).
siteSurfaceRates.methodology: Surface groups use the `rv_surface_type` text field with keyword rules (e.g. paved/concrete/asphalt → Concrete Pad; gravel/dirt/crushed/road base/decomposed granite/caliche → Loose Gravel; grass/turf/field → Grass or Field). Unmapped, blank, or mixed values are excluded.
siteSurfaceRates.noData: No rows matched the cohort for this chart.
siteSurfaceRates.loadError: Could not load site surface rates chart data.

amenitiesByPropertyPct.subtitle: Share of distinct campgrounds (property name, state, city) with each amenity on at least one row
amenitiesByPropertyPct.intro: Each percentage is the count of distinct properties with that amenity present on any qualifying Campspot row, divided by all distinct properties in the cohort. A row qualifies only when 2025 occupancy and 2025 ARDR (average retail daily rate 2025 column) pass standard bands; a property is included if it has a property name and U.S. state in one of the five RV industry regions.
amenitiesByPropertyPct.methodology: Present means yes/y/true/1 (case-insensitive) on the column, except 50 amp: also counted when `electrical_hook_up` text contains “50 amp”. Columns: hot_tub_sauna, pool, electrical_hook_up (50 amp only), sewer_hook_up, water_hookup.
amenitiesByPropertyPct.noData: No properties matched the cohort for this chart.
amenitiesByPropertyPct.loadError: Could not load amenities chart data.

amenitiesByAvgAdr.chartTitle: Amenities by Avg. Retail Daily Rate
amenitiesByAvgAdr.subtitle: RV sites were only used in determining these differences, tent sites and lodging units are excluded
amenitiesByAvgAdr.intro: For each amenity, mean 2025 ARDR (avg. retail daily rate) is computed separately for RV site rows with the amenity present (yes/y/true/1) versus without. Only rows classified as RV sites and in the five U.S. regions are included. ARDR uses the average retail daily rate 2025 column only.
amenitiesByAvgAdr.methodology: RV classification uses the same unit-type rules as the Unit Type charts (unit_type, description, property_name). Rows with missing ADR or ADR outside the standard band are excluded from both splits.
amenitiesByAvgAdr.noData: No RV site rows matched the cohort for this chart.
amenitiesByAvgAdr.loadError: Could not load amenities by ADR chart data.

stateAdrChoropleth.chartTitle: Avg. Retail Daily Rate by State
stateAdrChoropleth.subtitle: Before Taxes and Booking Fees
stateAdrChoropleth.intro: Mean 2025 ARDR (avg. retail daily rate) by U.S. state for Campspot rows in the five RV industry regions. Each row contributes at most once per state when average retail daily rate 2025 parses within the regional ARDR band (RV/Tent $10–$3k; Lodging $10–$10k on this map). Occupancy is not required for this map.
stateAdrChoropleth.methodology: Color scale spans roughly {low}–{high} per night for display; values below or above are shown as the lightest or darkest color. States with no qualifying rows are labeled N/A. States with at least one row but fewer than {minN} qualifying rows are labeled insufficient data and shown in neutral gray. Alaska and Hawaii follow the same rules; Hawaii is drawn in a small inset to the left of Alaska in the lower-left corner.
stateAdrChoropleth.calloutAria: State labels for smaller eastern states

rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.subtitle: Avg. Retail Daily Rates Are Before Taxes and Booking Fees
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.notAffectedByUnitFilter: Not filtered by the RV Sites / Tent Sites / Lodging toggle above; this section always reflects RV site rows only (same cohort as choosing RV Sites).
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.intro: Left: share of RV site rows (five U.S. regions) by `rv_parking` text mapped to Back-in, Pull thru, or Not Listed. Right: mean 2025 ARDR for Pull thru and Back-in rows only; Not Listed is excluded from the rate comparison. Only rows with 2025 occupancy and 2025 ARDR from the average retail daily rate 2025 column in standard bands are counted.
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.methodology: RV rows use the same unit-type classification as other RV-only charts. Pull thru matches pull-through / pull thru wording; Back-in matches back-in / back in; anything else or blank is Not Listed. Distribution and rate bars share the same qualifying row gate.
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.noData: No RV site rows matched the cohort.
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.noDistributionData: No rows to show in the distribution chart.
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.noRateData: No Pull thru or Back-in rows with positive ADR for the rate chart.
rvSiteTypesDistributionRates.loadError: Could not load RV site types charts data.

usagePanel.title: Usage panel
usagePanel.subtitle: Comps v2 web research tallies and admin AI Gateway usage (deep enrich, Site Builder, etc.) for the selected range.
usagePanel.partialDataWarning: Some data sources failed to load; totals may be incomplete. Check server logs.
usagePanel.compsV2Note: Tavily, Firecrawl, and web-row geocoding counts from persisted discovery runs (search and gap-fill). Past-report geocodes are not included.
usagePanel.adminAiNote: Token usage from features that log to admin_ai_usage_events (comps v2 deep enrich per model call, Site Builder images, etc.).
usagePanel.noCompsData: No comps v2 usage in this range.
usagePanel.noAiData: No AI usage events in this range.
usagePanel.recentAiEventsNote: Up to 100 most recent events in the selected range (after feature filter). Comps v2 deep enrich logs one row per condense and extraction call.

cceCosts.title: Cost Explorer
cceCosts.subtitle: Marshall & Swift Commercial Cost Explorer data – search and filter cost estimates

filters.outdoorHospitalityScopeHint: Show only Marshall & Swift rows relevant to glamping, RV resorts, campgrounds, marinas, and landscape/resort hotels (lodging, camping, marinas). Turn off to see all occupancies and component sections.
filters.extractionQualityFlagHint: Filter component rows where extraction_flags contains this key (requires add-cce-component-costs-flags migration). Use API query extraction_flag=… until UI wiring.

component.noData: No component costs yet. Run extraction (includes Wall Costs, Doors, etc.):

catalog.noData: No catalog units yet. Run extraction:

table.noData: No cost data yet. Run the migration and extraction script:
table.noDataGeneric: No data yet. Upload a Marshall & Swift PDF above and run extraction to load data.
table.noSearchResults: No results found. Try another search or adjust your filters.

compsV2.title: Comps v2 (beta)
compsV2.subtitle: Multi-source discovery: market data, past reports, optional web gap fill, and deep enrichment.
compsV2.locationLinePlaceholder: 123 Main St, Austin, TX 78701 or Austin, TX
compsV2.addressNoApiKeyHint: Add NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY for address suggestions (US & Canada).
compsV2.addressSuggestionsHint: Start typing for Google Places suggestions (US & Canada).
compsV2.previewMapLoadError: Map could not load. Check your Google Maps API key.
compsV2.previewMapEnterLocation: Enter an address or city and state to preview the search center and radius on the map.
compsV2.maxMergedResultsHint: After dedupe, at most {max} properties are returned. Each enabled database source loads up to {rowLimit} rows before merge, so dense areas may hit this cap before all nearby rows are seen.
compsV2.qualityTiers: Quality tiers (ARDR range for market data)
compsV2.sourcesRequired: Select at least one data source.
compsV2.sourcesGlampingStrictFootnote: Glamping-only discovery does not query RoverPass or Campspot, even if they stay selected. Add RV or Campground to include those tables.
compsV2.discoveryProgressHint: While discovery runs, the server streams pipeline phases and web-research tallies so steps and warnings update in real time (time remaining is only a rough fallback if streaming is unavailable).
compsV2.discoveryCancelledBanner: Discovery was cancelled. No new results were applied.
compsV2.discoveryLiveWebTallies: Web research so far: Tavily {tavilyDone}/{tavilyPlanned} queries · {tavilyRaw} API rows · Firecrawl {fcEnriched}/{fcAttempted} pages · Geocoding Google {gGeo} / Nominatim {nGeo} (distance pass: {distHits}/{distAttempts})
compsV2.discoveryWebSubTavilyLive: {completed}/{planned} Tavily queries complete · {raw} raw API rows (budget up to {maxQueries} queries × {resultsPerQuery} rows each).
compsV2.discoveryWebSubTavilyLiveErrors: {count, plural, one {# Tavily query error} other {# Tavily query errors}} — web yield may be reduced.
compsV2.tavilyStreamingErrorsTitle: Some Tavily searches failed during this run
compsV2.tavilyStreamingErrorsHint: Discovery can still succeed with partial web results. Details:
compsV2.discoverySourceTimingsTitle: Market source durations (wall-clock ms per source)
compsV2.discoveryWebSubTavily: Tavily: up to {maxQueries} separate searches, each requesting up to {resultsPerQuery} raw result rows; answers and pages are filtered for single-property relevance and to drop listicles or directory hubs.
compsV2.discoveryWebSubFirecrawl: Firecrawl: up to {firecrawlPages} property URLs scraped for fuller text when the API key is set; at 0, rows stay on Tavily snippets only.
compsV2.discoveryWebSubGeocode: Geocoding: uses coordinates from scraped page data (JSON-LD, map links) when present; otherwise Google Places (find place) biased to your search area, then street-style Geocoding API queries, then OpenStreetMap (Nominatim), for map pins and distance from the anchor (capped per run).
compsV2.discoveryWebSubDedupe: Merge: new names and URLs are deduped against your current discovery list before property-type, ADR, tier, and glamping filters.
compsV2.resultsPropertiesFound: {count, plural, one {1 Property Found} other {# Properties Found}}
compsV2.resultsPropertiesFoundWithTotal: {count, plural, one {1 Property Found} other {# Properties Found}} ({total} total)
compsV2.noResults: No candidates matched this discovery run. Widen the radius, adjust property types or data sources, and run discovery again.
compsV2.noResultsAfterFilters: No rows match your current search or filters. Clear the search box or filters to see properties.
compsV2.gapFillOnlyDisabled: Run discovery first so search anchor and state are set.
compsV2.webResearchSummaryTavily: Tavily: {raw} API rows → {filtered} kept after relevance + specific-property filters (skipped {skipped} listicles/hub pages).
compsV2.webResearchAnchorNote: Web queries used radius language centered on {city}, {state}.
compsV2.webResearchDistanceGeocoded: {hits} web rows were geocoded for distance (Nominatim; capped per run).
compsV2.webResearchTavilyNoKey: TAVILY_API_KEY is not set — Tavily web search was skipped. Add the key to run web gap-fill.
compsV2.webResearchTavilyStats: Tavily: {raw} raw result rows from the API, {relevance} passed relevance, {emitted} unique properties emitted (cap {cap}), {queries} queries completed.
compsV2.webResearchFirecrawlNoKey: FIRECRAWL_API_KEY is not set — Firecrawl page scrapes were skipped; rows stay on Tavily snippets only.
compsV2.webResearchFirecrawlStats: Firecrawl: {enriched} pages enriched of {attempted} attempts ({blocked} blocked URL policy, {failed} failed or too short).
compsV2.webResearchFirecrawlSkippedZero: Firecrawl pages to scrape is set to 0 — only Tavily snippets are used.
compsV2.webResearchAfterFilters: {count} web-sourced properties passed property-type, ARDR, tier, and glamping filters and were merged.
compsV2.webResearchCostIntro: Reduce Tavily and Firecrawl usage with lower numbers (quality may drop in sparse markets).
compsV2.webResearchCostBudgetNote: Budget used: up to {tavilyQ} Tavily queries × {tavilyRows} SERP rows each; up to {firecrawlCap} Firecrawl page scrapes.
compsV2.deepProgressHint: Each property uses your official URL when present, Google Places (when configured), several targeted web searches, then a high-accuracy model. Expect one to several minutes per batch.
compsV2.deepStepFetch: Fetching site, Google listing & web research
compsV2.errorGeocode: Could not geocode that location. Check the address or set a Google Maps API key.
compsV2.errorSelectPropertyKind: Select at least one property type.
compsV2.errorDeepEnrichMinCount: Select at least {min} properties for deep enrichment (max {max}).
compsV2.errorGapFillCityRequired: Search anchor city is missing. Run discovery again with a full address or place line.
compsV2.errorGapFillStateRequired: A valid 2-letter state is required for gap-fill. Run discovery again from an address that includes state.
compsV2.errorGapFillExistingTooLarge: Too many comps to send for gap-fill (max {max}). Narrow filters or reduce results.
compsV2.errorDiscoveryStreamCorrupt: Discovery response was incomplete or invalid. Try again; if it keeps happening, contact support with your correlation id.
compsV2.errorEnrichApiItemsRequired: Deep enrichment requires a non-empty items array in the request body.
compsV2.errorEnrichApiMinItems: Deep enrichment needs at least 3 properties (same as the admin UI).
compsV2.errorEnrichApiMaxItems: Select at most 5 properties for deep enrichment.
compsV2.errorEnrichApiNoValidItems: No valid items: each entry needs a property name.
compsV2.errorEnrichApiInsufficientValid: At least 3 properties with names are required for deep enrichment.
compsV2.errorEnrichApiInternal: Deep enrichment failed on the server. Try again or check logs.
compsV2.summaryCompositionCaption: {rawRows} rows from market tables and past reports before merge and dedupe.
compsV2.summaryCompositionWebHint: Web research: the count is properties added from Tavily (and optional Firecrawl) after filters and dedupe.
compsV2.resultsSearchPlaceholder: Search by property, city, state, address, or source…
compsV2.colLocationHint: City and state; web research may add a parsed street or place line below.
compsV2.colMiHint: Miles from your search center. A dash means unknown; those rows sort last.
compsV2.colMiUnknown: Distance not available for this row.
compsV2.colOccupancyHint: Market occupancy when reported by Hipcamp, Campspot, or RoverPass (shown as %).
compsV2.colOccupancyUnknown: Occupancy not reported for this source.
compsV2.resultsMapPartialCoords: Map shows {shown} of {total} properties with coordinates. Web-sourced rows only get pins when geocoded (best-effort after discovery).
compsV2.resultsMapWebGeocoding: Locating web research properties on the map…

sitemap.title: Sitemap
sitemap.metaDescription: Browse all pages and resources on Sage Outdoor Advisory. Find guides, glossary terms, glamping properties map, and more.
